heading: Hi, I'm Romand.
I build solutions
for complex problems.
bio: I build full-stack web applications and work closely with product, QA, and engineering teams. My day usually involves fixing bugs, building new features, and a lot of coffee. I genuinely enjoy what I do, especially that feeling when things finally work.
I like clarity. I like systems. I like when things make sense.

skills.heading: Tools I know.
skills.subheading: I work across the full stack, from designing interfaces to building APIs to deploying on the cloud. Here's what I reach for depending on the job.

items.title: Programming Languages
items.description: These are the languages I am most confident with. JavaScript and TypeScript for the web, Python for data and scripting, Java and C# for enterprise systems, and Dart for mobile. I'm comfortable switching between them.

items.title: Frontend Development
items.description: I build interfaces that are fast, clean, and easy to use. I work mostly with Vue.js and React depending on the project. I care about details, spacing, transitions, responsiveness, which are the things users notice without knowing why.

items.title: Backend Development
items.description: I design and build APIs and services that are reliable, maintainable, and easy to scale. I've worked with Node.js and Nest.js for JavaScript backends, Django and FastAPI for Python, and Spring Boot for Java. I like systems that are predictable and well-structured.

items.title: Databases
items.description: I work with both relational and non-relational databases depending on what the project needs. PostgreSQL and MySQL for structured data, MongoDB for flexibility, Redis for caching and queues, and Supabase and Firebase for projects that need a backend fast.

items.title: DevOps & Tools
items.description: I'm comfortable with the full deployment side of things, containerizing with Docker, orchestrating with Kubernetes, setting up CI/CD pipelines, and working with message brokers like Kafka and RabbitMQ. I also build mobile apps with Flutter and handle payments with Stripe.

work.heading: Things I have
work.subheading: A few projects I'm proud of, from university work to personal builds. Each one taught me something new.

projects.name: Peertube
projects.subtitle: Peer Tutoring System
projects.description: A scalable peer tutoring platform with session management, financial dashboards, refund and report features, and payment integration.
bullets: Developed tutor, student, and admin workflows with session management, financial dashboards, refund and report features, and Stripe PromptPay integration.
Integrated full-text search with indexing, notifications (Firebase), email (Resend), Google OAuth, real-time chat, and automated tasks with cron jobs.
Optimized performance with Supabase queues, server-side prefetching, and SSR for SEO.

projects.name: Trideo
projects.subtitle: Learning Analytic System
projects.description: A full-stack video learning platform with admin dashboards to track user activities — visits, views, and completions.
bullets: Built full-stack video learning platform with admin dashboards to track user activities (visits, views, completions).
Implemented Google OAuth (Passport.js), video uploads via Cloudinary and Multer, and event streaming with managed Kafka (Confluent Cloud). Used Prisma ORM with MongoDB Atlas.
Deployed frontend on Vercel, backend on Render, Kafka worker on Fly.io.

projects.name: Asset Borrowing
projects.subtitle: Mobile App
projects.description: A mobile app for borrowing and returning assets, with role-based workflows for users, lecturers, and staff.
bullets: Users can book, borrow, return assets, with lecturer roles managing approvals and staff managing takeout and returns.
Implemented secure JWT-based authentication, email OTP verification, and automated asset status updates with node-schedule.
Used Cloudinary and Multer for image uploads.

projects.name: Salary Prediction
projects.subtitle: ML Model
projects.description: A machine learning model that predicts salaries using an Artificial Neural Network, enhanced with ensemble and dimensionality reduction techniques.
bullets: Developed a salary prediction model using ANN, enhanced with bagging and PCA for improved performance and robustness.

experience.heading: Building things.
experience.subheading: I've worked across universities, banks, and startups — writing code, supporting teams, and figuring out how real software gets built and shipped.

items.description: Worked in an Agile team handling tickets via Jira across the full dev lifecycle. Delivered features, bug fixes, and customer-driven hotfixes to production. Built LLM-powered pipelines and microservice workflows for scalable data processing.
Supported students in Mobile and Web development labs, offering hands-on guidance and strengthening problem-solving and communication skills through diverse one-on-one interactions.

Delivered end-user support for software, hardware, and network issues including system setup, installation, and maintenance. Collaborated with developers and IT teams to troubleshoot problems remotely and on-site.

about.heading: Beyond the
bio: I like the feeling when something clicks into place. I like systems that make sense, code that's easy to read, and teams that communicate well.
Outside of work, I'm probably tinkering with a side project, playing too much chess, or trying to understand how something works just because I'm curious.

facts.label: Education
facts.value: B.E. Computer Engineering, Mae Fah Luang University

facts.label: Off The Clock
facts.value: Playing chess, tinkering with side projects, watching edutainment videos

facts.label: Current Interest
facts.value: How LLMs are changing the way software gets built

facts.label: Also Me
facts.value: The harder the problem, the sweeter the moment when I finally solve it

connect.heading: Let's build
connect.subheading: Got a project in mind, want to collaborate, or just say hi? My inbox is always open.
